reference_module.purpose: Summary of key GOV.UK Design System guidelines including overarching principles, component usage and colour rules.

principles.rule: Design government services using GOV.UK styles, components and patterns to ensure consistency【635201063113150†L167-L172】.
Use the design system to learn from the research and experience of other teams and avoid repeating work【635201063113150†L169-L171】.
Follow the Government Design Principles, the GOV.UK Service Manual and the GOV.UK style and content design guidance when using the design system【635201063113150†L229-L234】.

css_cascade_rules.rule: Do not use the CSS `!important` flag in ResearchOps stylesheets. Resolve precedence through correct stylesheet ownership, selector design, source order and component boundaries instead.
When a style requires higher precedence, change the cascade deliberately. Do not override the cascade with `!important`.

colour_guidelines.rule: Always use the GOV.UK colour palette【529411222321622†L196-L204】.
Ensure that the contrast ratio of text and interactive elements meets WCAG 2.2 success criterion 1.4.3 (minimum)【529411222321622†L200-L203】.
Assign functional colours using the `govuk-functional-colour` Sass function rather than copying hex values; this automatically applies the most recent palette【529411222321622†L206-L223】.
Only use functional colour variables in their intended context; otherwise reference the GOV.UK web palette directly【529411222321622†L224-L226】.
Use the focus colour (`#ffdd00`) only to indicate focus states and ensure it has sufficient contrast【529411222321622†L253-L257】.
Use the error colour (`#ca3535`) for error messages and the success colour (`#0f7a52`) for success messages【529411222321622†L259-L265】.
Use the hover colour (`#cecece`) for input hover states【529411222321622†L267-L269】.

component.name: back-link
component.when_to_use: Always include the back link component on GOV.UK question pages and consider including it on other pages in multi‑page transactions when it helps users【70625681417284†L260-L266】.
component.when_not_to_use: Never use the back link component together with the breadcrumbs component; conduct user research to decide which navigation aid is more helpful【70625681417284†L268-L272】.
component.how_it_works: Place the back link at the top of a page before the <main> element so that the ‘Skip to main content’ link skips navigation links【70625681417284†L274-L278】.
component.behavior: Ensure the link takes users back to the previous page in the state they last saw it and provide a fallback when JavaScript is not available【70625681417284†L280-L284】.
component.variation: When the back link appears on a dark background, use the `govuk-back-link--inverse` modifier class and ensure the background colour contrasts with the white link by at least 4.5:1【70625681417284†L344-L352】.
